steps.title: Connect the conclusion
steps.content: The passage says Spanish words in Cisneros's story can be understood from surrounding English context. Since readers unfamiliar with Spanish can easily read both works, Castillo's approach must work the same basic way.

steps.title: Reject mismatched phrases
steps.content: Choice A says the approach is unfamiliar, which would not support easy reading of both works. Choice B is grammatically and logically off target because frequency is not the issue. Choice C does not make sense as a comparison between approaches. The remaining choice expresses the needed likeness. The answer is D.
